stem: A freight company charges $2.15 per pound to ship goods overseas. A merchant wants to ship a crate weighing 350 kilograms. What is the total shipping cost in dollars? (1 kilogram = 2.205 pounds)
explanation:
Step 1: Convert the crate's weight from kilograms to pounds.
$$350 \text{ kg} \times \frac{2.205 \text{ lb}}{1 \text{ kg}} = 771.75 \text{ lb}$$

Step 2: Multiply by the shipping rate.
$$771.75 \text{ lb} \times \frac{\$2.15}{1 \text{ lb}} = \$1{,}659.26 \approx \$1{,}659$$

The total shipping cost is **$1,659**.

distractorRationale.A: Inverts the kilogram-to-pound conversion: student divides by 2.205 instead of multiplying. $350 \div 2.205 \approx 158.73$ lb; $158.73 \times 2.15 \approx \$341$.
distractorRationale.B: Forgets to convert kilograms to pounds: student applies the shipping rate directly to the weight in kilograms. $350 \times \$2.15 = \$752.50 \approx \$753$.
distractorRationale.C: Uses a wrong conversion factor: student substitutes 1 kg = 2.0 lb instead of 2.205 lb. $350 \times 2.0 = 700$ lb; $700 \times \$2.15 = \$1{,}505$.
